problems.title: List Operations
problems.hint:
nums = list(map(int, input().split()))
print('Sum:', sum(nums))
print('Max:', max(nums))
print('Min:', min(nums))
print('Sorted:', sorted(nums))

steps.text: Read the input line and split it into individual string tokens.
Convert each token to an integer using map(int, ...) and wrap in list().
Use built-in functions sum(), max(), min() to compute the values.
Use sorted() to get an ascending list and print all four lines.

problems.title: List Slicing & Reversal
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: Space-separated integers
Line 2: Slice start index
Line 3: Slice end index
problems.outputFormat:
Original: [n1, n2, ...]
Reversed: [n1, n2, ...]
Slice: [n1, n2, ...]
problems.hint:
nums = list(map(int, input().split()))
start = int(input())
end = int(input())
print('Original:', nums)
print('Reversed:', nums[::-1])
print('Slice:', nums[start:end])

steps.text: Read the list of integers from line 1.
Read start and end indices from lines 2 and 3.
Use nums[::-1] to reverse the list.
Use nums[start:end] for the slice and print all three lines.

problems.title: List Comprehension Filter
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: Space-separated integers
Line 2: A threshold number
problems.outputFormat:
Even numbers: [n1, n2, ...]
Above threshold: [n1, n2, ...]
Squares of evens: [n1, n2, ...]
problems.hint:
nums = list(map(int, input().split()))
threshold = int(input())
evens = [x for x in nums if x % 2 == 0]
above = [x for x in nums if x > threshold]
squares = [x**2 for x in evens]

steps.text: Read the list and the threshold value.
Use a list comprehension to filter even numbers.
Use another comprehension to find numbers above the threshold.
Square each even number with x**2 in a third comprehension.

problems.title: Tuple Operations
problems.hint:
vals = tuple(input().split(','))
print('Tuple:', vals)
print('Length:', len(vals))
print('First:', vals[0])
print('Last:', vals[-1])

steps.text: Read the input, split by comma, and convert to a tuple.
Print the tuple as-is using print().
Use len() to get the length.
Access first element with index 0 and last with index -1.

problems.title: Tuple Unpacking and Methods
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: Comma-separated integers
Line 2: A value to search for
problems.outputFormat:
Count of <value>: <n>
Index of <value>: <n>
Max: <n>
Min: <n>
problems.hint:
nums = tuple(map(int, input().split(',')))
val = int(input())
print(f'Count of {val}:', nums.count(val))
print(f'Index of {val}:', nums.index(val))
print('Max:', max(nums))
print('Min:', min(nums))

steps.text: Read and convert the input into a tuple of integers.
Read the search value from line 2.
Use .count() to count occurrences and .index() to find the position.
Use max() and min() on the tuple and print all four lines.

problems.title: Student Records with Tuples
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: Number of students (n)
Next n lines: name,grade
problems.hint:
n = int(input())
students = []
for _ in range(n):
    name, grade = input().split(',')
    students.append((name, int(grade)))
highest = max(students, key=lambda x: x[1])
lowest = min(students, key=lambda x: x[1])
avg = sum(g for _,g in students) // n

steps.text: Read n and then loop n times, splitting each line into (name, grade) tuples.
Store tuples in a list.
Use max() and min() with a lambda key to find highest and lowest grades.
Calculate average with integer division and print all three lines.

problems.title: Team Skills Analyzer
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: Comma-separated skills for Team A
Line 2: Comma-separated skills for Team B
problems.hint:
team_a = set(input().split(','))
team_b = set(input().split(','))
# Use |, &, - for set ops
# sorted() to alphabetize
# ', '.join(...) to format

steps.text: Read both input lines and split each by comma — you'll have two lists of skills.
Convert each list to a set. Sets ignore duplicates and unlock set math operations.
Compute four things: union (|) for all skills, intersection (&) for shared, and difference (-) twice for each team's unique skills.
Sort each result with sorted() so the output is alphabetical and consistent.
Format and print each line, wrapping the sorted values in curly braces { }.

testCases.label: Case #2
expected: All skills: {css, html, js, react, vue}

testCases.label: Case #3
expected: All skills: {aws, django, docker, flask, python, sql}

testCases.label: Case #5
expected: All skills: {cpp, go, java, kotlin, rust, swift}

problems.title: Duplicate Remover & Set Builder
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: Comma-separated student IDs (may have duplicates)
Line 2: A single student ID to search for
problems.outputFormat:
Unique students: <count>
Duplicates removed: <count>
Duplicate IDs: {id1, id2, ...}
Enrolled: Yes / No
problems.hint:
ids = input().split(',')
search = input().strip()
unique = set(ids)
# dupes_removed = len(ids) - len(unique)
# track seen set for duplicate IDs

steps.text: Read the first line and split by comma — keep the original list, its length matters for counting duplicates.
Convert the list to a set to get unique values. Size difference between list and set = duplicates removed.
Find which IDs appeared more than once by iterating through the original list while tracking what you've already seen.
Read the second input as the search ID, then use 'in' on the unique set to check enrollment.
Print all four output lines, sorting the duplicate IDs set before formatting.

problems.title: Course Prerequisite Checker
problems.inputFormat:
Line 1: Comma-separated prerequisite courses required
Line 2: Comma-separated courses the student completed
problems.hint:
required  = set(input().split(','))
completed = set(input().split(','))
missing = required - completed
extra   = completed - required
# Completion = int(len(completed & required) / len(required) * 100)

steps.text: Read both lines and convert each to a set — one for required prerequisites, one for completed courses.
Use set difference (required - completed) to find what the student is still missing.
Use the reverse difference (completed - required) to find extra courses taken beyond prerequisites.
If the missing set is empty the student can enroll — otherwise they cannot.
Completion % = required courses the student completed ÷ total required × 100. Round down with int().
Print all four lines, sorting missing and extra sets before formatting in curly braces.
